dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a possible cardiac arrest call.
Hi, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 tonight?
My husband was found unresponsive on the floor-no breathing and no pulse.
Mr. Thompson, can you squeeze my fingers or look at me?
He never moved or responded. It was like he was already gone.
Fire rescue is currently using a BVM with OPA. Let me take over airway management.
Go ahead. They were using good technique, so continue with the same.
Absolutely no spontaneous breathing detected. We'll need to continue BVM-assisted ventilation.
No central nor peripheral pulse detected. CPR is necessary.
No pulse is detectable, even with several repeated attempts.
No spontaneous respirations-respiratory effort is zero.
No blood pressure is detectable at all-absent completely.
The blood glucose is 324 mg/dL-very high.
Oxygen saturation is not measurable-no lung function is occurring.
Continuous asystole is depicted on 4-lead EKG since we arrived.
Did Mr. Thompson seem to experience any type of pain beforehand?
He was just resting after dinner and seemed fine otherwise.
The wife said he had a history of hypertension and diabetic type 1. Anything unusual lately?
He was on metformin, glypizide, aspirin, lisinopril, lovastatin, and trazodone.
Family said he worked in the yard and then had dinner around 7 p.m. before going to sleep.
He finished dinner just before going to his room-nothing unusual happening.
No motor or verbal responses. Glasgow Coma Scale of 3-unresponsive in all aspects.
His eyes are fixed and dilated on both sides. No light reaction is observed.
Confirming continuous 4-lead monitoring-still flatline asystole.
Beginning manual CPR now. Paced by the machine. Ready when I am.
I've engaged the AutoPulse. Compressions are now automated at correct rate and depth.
The AutoPulse is stabilized and in place.
We're now performing oral intubation with inline capnography to confirm.
Intraosseous access placed in the right humeral head-per protocol, no issues noted.
Administering 1mg of epinephrine (1 in 10,000) through the IO line now-rapid push.
Capnography shows correct ET tube placement. Confirmed with positive waveform.
Giving a second 1mg of epinephrine via IO route-same dilution.
2mg of naloxone has been administered IO.
Delivering 50mEq of sodium bicarbonate via IO now.
All steps have been executed per protocol, and medical control has stated to terminate resuscitation.
No pulse-still unmeasurable.
No breathing. We remain on BVM-assisted only.
全くの血圧なし-依然として検出不可です。
Glucose remains at 324 mg/dL despite ongoing resuscitation.
Oxygen saturation is still undetectable-no improvement noted.
The EKG rhythm remains consistent asystole-no evidence of returnable rhythm detected.
Still no changes in pain behavior-patient remains unresponsive.
All resuscitative efforts were completed per protocol. Orders to cease have been received.
Scene is cleared and stabilized per on-line medical direction.
No need to transport-residency will be documented in the field as per protocol.
